mnemonics: FABIO - Files Are Bits In Order (for file system structure)
VIC - Volatile Inode Cache (to remember caching purpose)
DAD - Directories Are Data (to remember they are just special files)
keyPoints: File system implementation involves on-disk structures (like superblocks, inodes, data blocks) and in-memory structures (like mount tables, open-file tables)
Allocation methods (contiguous, linked, indexed) represent different strategies for storing file data blocks
Directory implementation and free space management are critical components that affect performance and reliability
commonMistakes: Confusing inodes with file names (inodes store metadata, names are in directories)
Assuming contiguous allocation is efficient for general use (it suffers from external fragmentation)
Forgetting that directory entries are simply mappings from names to inode numbers
